market_summary: S&P 500 at ~7,124 on April 21 (up from 7,041 April 17 close); April 20 saw a -0.24% decline on renewed Iran tensions before recovering Tuesday. Nasdaq snapped a 13-day winning streak (longest since 1992). Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 18-19, reversing the brief April 17 opening. Fragile ceasefire expires April 23; Tehran vowing retaliation after US vessel seizure. IEA describes the disruption as the largest oil supply shock in global market history. March 2026 CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM (highest since early 2024); April CPI due May 12 and expected to rise further. S&P 500 is approximately 12.5% above March 30 lows. Eight of eleven S&P sectors reporting year-over-year earnings growth, led by Information Technology, Materials, and Financials. CRITICAL MILESTONE: CSPX $250 ADD finally executed on April 20 (confirmed by position growth 1.4745 -> 1.8025 shares), resolving the 9-report operational failure.
macro_themes: 1. IRAN/HORMUZ: Re-closure April 18-19 is the dominant macro event. Ceasefire expires April 23. Tehran vowing retaliation after vessel seizure. Stagflation and recession risk elevated — IEA 'largest supply disruption in history.' US-China tensions rising as blockade cuts Chinese oil imports. 2. INFLATION: March CPI +3.3% YoY; April CPI likely higher given Hormuz re-closure. Fed constrained — JPM sees no cuts in 2026, possible 25bps hike Q3 2027. Rate cuts deferred to H2 2026 at earliest. 3. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China 125%) continues. US-China relations strained by Hormuz blockade impact on Chinese oil. 4. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: Hyperscalers collectively committing $200B+ to AI infrastructure in 2026. $1T in confirmed chip demand through 2027. Blackwell sold out through mid-2026 at $40K/GPU. Vera Rubin (H2 2026) is next inflection catalyst. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Rotation from pure growth to quality value. Tech recovering from Q1 lows. Energy was YTD leader but oil profits evaporating as Hormuz dynamics shift back to closure.
portfolio_health: Grade: B- (77/100). NAV $4,291.59. Week return -0.74% vs S&P 500 (April 17-20) -0.24% = -0.50pp underperformance, fully attributable to commission drag from CSPX ADD execution ($2-3 friction) and minor CSPX price pullback from $766.84 entry to $762.96 close. MILESTONE: CSPX $250 ADD executed April 20 — 9-report operational failure resolved. CSPX weight improved from 26.2% to 33.96% of NAV. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +2.40%. NEW CONCERN: ASML crosses into DETERIORATING territory — new China export threat April 20, China mix 36%->19% (approaching 15% invalidation threshold), stock fell 6% on Q1 beat. EXIT recommended. All other positions have intact or strengthening theses. Upcoming catalysts: AMZN Q1 earnings April 29, MA Q1 earnings April 30, FOMC April 28-29. WHT YTD: $3.33 withheld (MA dividend only).

S&P 500 near all-time highs, AI capex supercycle intact, CSPX IE-domiciled accumulating structure remains WHT-optimal anchor for Lebanese resident.

Q1 2026 earnings April 29 at 5:30 PM ET. AWS +24% YoY in Q4 2025 — fastest growth in 13 quarters; $15B AI run rate. TD Cowen Buy at $300 target (+21% upside). Wide Q1 operating income guidance ($16.5-21.5B) creates execution risk — hold pre-earnings; no adds. RSI elevated.

Iran re-closed Hormuz April 18-19; ceasefire expires April 23; Tehran vowing retaliation. IEA: largest oil supply disruption in history. GLD at $442.09 is 5.3% above $420 invalidation trigger. Institutional targets: Goldman $5,400 XAU end-2026, JPM $4K-$6.3K. 4-week risk-on clock fully reset. All four invalidation conditions untriggered.

new_opportunities.rationale: TSMC is the foundational pick-and-shovel AI semiconductor play — fabricates all NVIDIA Blackwell and Vera Rubin chips, plus AAPL and AMD. 32% YoY revenue growth forecast for current quarter, 40%+ 2026 earnings growth, forward P/E 25 comparable to Nasdaq-100 but with materially higher growth rate. Natural complement to NVDA thesis. Dividend yield ~1.5% subject to Taiwan WHT (~21% treaty rate — modest drag). Research candidate only — not actionable until NAV >$5,000 AND CSPX at target AND ASML fully exited.
